title: Meta in talks to rent AI compute to Anthropic
summary: Meta is in early-stage negotiations to rent AI computing power from its data centers to AI startup Anthropic, with a potential deal worth up to $10 billion over two years. This deal underscores the acute scarcity of AI computing infrastructure and could provide Meta with a new revenue stream to offset its massive capital expenditures, while giving Anthropic access to critical compute resources. Anthropic proposed the deal in June 2026, and Meta is evaluating it; if signed, Anthropic would pay monthly and either party could exit early. Talks are preliminary and may not lead to an agreement.
context: Meta plans to spend up to $145 billion this year, much of it on AI and data centers. The potential deal highlights how AI startups are scrambling for compute capacity amid a global GPU shortage, while large tech firms seek to monetize their infrastructure investments.
